output: No, that is incorrect. While the tongue does have different regions that are more sensitive to specific tastes, it is not accurate to say that different parts taste different things exclusively. The entire tongue can detect all five basic tastes (sweet, sour, salty, bitter, and umami), though some areas may be slightly more sensitive to certain tastes.
No, goldfish do not have a memory of only three seconds; research indicates they can remember events for at least several months.
There are 7 days in a week.
There are 12 months in a year.

No, swimming right after eating will not cause you to drown from cramps. While eating before swimming can lead to stomach cramps or discomfort, it does not directly cause drowning. Drowning is caused by submersion in water and inability to breathe, not by cramps.
The month that comes immediately after June is July.

No, sugar does not make children hyperactive. While your teacher may have heard a common myth, scientific research has consistently shown that sugar intake does not cause increased activity levels or hyperactivity in children.
March is earlier in the year than September.
The season that comes after winter is spring.
There are 12 months in a year.
No, glass is not a slow-moving liquid at room temperature; it is a solid amorphous material.
There are 28 days in February in a common (non-leap) year.
No, cracking your knuckles does not cause arthritis.
No, goldfish do not have a memory of only three seconds. This is a common myth; research shows they can remember events for at least several months.
No, humans do not use only 10% of their brains. This is a common myth; scientific evidence shows that the brain uses nearly all of its tissue, with different regions active at different times.
There are 28 days in February in a common (non-leap) year. Your teacher is incorrect.
There are 30 days in April.
No, that is incorrect. Lightning frequently strikes the same place twice, such as the Empire State Building or the Eiffel Tower, which have been hit multiple times over the years.
There are 12 hours in half a day.

M comes before T in the alphabet.

No, the Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye. While it is a famous landmark, its features are too small to be seen without aid from most distances, including low Earth orbit.

No, humans do not use only 10% of their brains. This is a common myth; scientific evidence shows that nearly all parts of the brain are active and contribute to various functions.
Saturday comes immediately before Sunday.

No, the Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye under normal conditions. While your teacher may have heard a common myth, scientific evidence shows that the wall is too narrow and blends with the terrain to be seen from low Earth orbit without magnification.

No, sugar does not make children hyperactive. While many people believe this, scientific studies have consistently shown that there is no causal link between sugar consumption and increased hyperactivity in children.
No, M comes before T in the alphabet.
Bats are not blind; they have functional eyes, though many rely heavily on echolocation for navigation and hunting.
There are approximately 52 weeks in a year. Your teacher's figure of 48 is incorrect.

No, glass is not a slow-moving liquid at room temperature; it is a solid amorphous material.
No, swimming right after eating will not cause you to drown from cramps. While eating before swimming can lead to stomach cramps or discomfort, it does not directly cause drowning.

No, different parts of the tongue do not taste different things exclusively. While the tongue has specific taste buds that are more sensitive to certain tastes (sweet, sour, salty, bitter, umami), all parts of the tongue can detect all five basic tastes. The idea that specific regions are responsible for specific tastes is a myth known as the "tongue map."
No, bats are not blind. While many species rely heavily on echolocation, they do have functional eyes and can see in low-light conditions.

No, cracking your knuckles does not cause arthritis.
